text.content:
SRX is an unofficial, free, fan-made game system for Foundry VTT v14. It ships mechanics as code and no rules text, stats, art, or logos — you bring your own data from the free SRX Character Builder.

Install by manifest URL (recommended)

  1. In the Foundry setup screen open Game Systems → Install System.
  2. Paste the SRX manifest URL into the Manifest URL field and click Install. (The current manifest URL is listed in the project README.)
  3. Create a new World and pick SRX — Shadowrun Edition X (Unofficial) as its game system.

Install manually (offline / development)

  1. Copy or symlink the system folder into your Foundry data at Data/systems/srx. The folder name must be srx to match the system id.
  2. Restart Foundry; the system appears in the list.

Compatibility

Built and verified against Foundry v14. It uses v14-era APIs (ApplicationV2 sheets, Data Models, Template Regions for area effects, Active Effects), so v13 and earlier are not supported.

The system ships with empty compendiums by design. Weapons, armour, gear, talents, spells, 'ware, vehicles, and the rest come from your copy of the free SRX Character Builder data.

Open the importer

As the GM, go to Game Settings → Configure Settings → SRX Content Import and click Import Catalog…. (Macro equivalent: game.srx.openCatalogImport().)

Point it at your builder data

  1. Click Choose files… and select one or more .txt.deploy files from your Character Builder Load Data folder — Weapons, Armor, Gear, Talents, Spells, Ware, Vehicles, and related tables.
  2. Click Import selected catalogs. The log reports how many items were created and how many were skipped as already present.
  3. Re-importing is safe: existing items are de-duplicated rather than doubled.

Imported catalog items land as world Items. Numeric effect columns become real Active Effects automatically, so adding, say, a piece of augmentation to an actor applies its bonuses without extra work.

Matrix talents

Hacking, Software, and Threading talents come from the same builder Talents file. Run game.srx.pickAndImportMatrixTalents() from a macro (or the script console) to create the Matrix talent set in its own folder; it is idempotent on re-import.

Characters & threats from JSON

The importer also accepts JSON exports: pregenerated characters and threat/NPC catalogs import as ready-to-play actors alongside the .txt.deploy catalogs.

SRX includes a guided character builder and a Karma advancement panel, both launched from the same place.

Launch the wizard

Create a new character actor and open its sheet. Click the Create / Advance button (the little user-gear icon the system adds to the sheet). A chooser offers two paths:

  • Build a new character — the priority wizard.
  • Advance an existing character — the Karma panel.

Macros: game.srx.openChargen(actor), game.srx.openAdvance(actor), or game.srx.openChargenChooser(actor).

Priority build

  1. Assign the SRX priorities across metatype, attributes, magic/resonance, skills, and resources.
  2. Spend the point pools each priority grants; the wizard validates legality as you go and flags an unbalanced build.
  3. Buy talents and knowledge from your imported catalog (import first — see the previous page).
  4. Commit. The wizard writes a legal, playable actor: attributes, skills, special ratings, and starting gear.

Karma advancement

After play, reopen Create / Advance → Advance. Spend earned Karma on attributes, skills, specialisations, knowledge, and talents. Every purchase is validated against caps and available Karma, and recorded in a running Karma ledger on the sheet so you can audit spend over a campaign.

Before your first session:

  • System installed and a world created on it.
  • Catalog imported from your builder data (weapons, gear, talents, spells at minimum).
  • Player characters built via the wizard or imported from pregen JSON, and assigned to players.
  • A few threats imported for opposition.
  • Automation level chosen under Game Settings → Configure Settings → SRX — how much the system auto-applies vs. prompts. The defaults are a good starting point.

Run the tutorial fight

New to the combat loop? Open the SRX Quick-Start — The 15-Minute Fight journal and run it once solo. It walks the attack → defend → resist → apply cycle end to end with two tokens.

Content & legal note

This system bundles no SRX or Shadowrun rules text, statistics, artwork, or logos. All catalog content comes from data you supply. Shadowrun is a trademark of The Topps Company, Inc.; this is an unofficial fan project, not affiliated with Topps, Catalyst Game Labs, or the SRX team, and it will be modified or withdrawn on request of any rights holder.
